cleavage agent: before trypsin digestion, all the protein samples were reduced and cysteine blocked using the reagents provided in the iTRAQ® Reagents-4plex Applications kit-Protein (AB Sciex).
collision energy: The fragmentation was carried out using higher-energy collision dissociation (HCD) with 50% normalized collision energy.
fragmentation method: The fragmentation was carried out using higher-energy collision dissociation (HCD).
instrument: identified using iTRAQ and LC-MS/MS analysis on an LTQ Orbitrap Velos mass spectrometer.
labeling: An omics approach for studying quantitative changes in brain proteome upon hypoxia insult following recovery was undertaken using iTRAQ-based LC-MS/MS approach.
reduction reagent: 50 mM HEPES (pH 7.8), 50 mM KCl, 300 mM NaCl, 0.1 M EDTA, 1 mM DTT, 10% (v/v) Glycerol and 1X protease inhibitor.
